title: paspalum nesiotes
intro: Paspalum nesiotes, also known as Kalo or Kalo Koa, is a type of tropical grass native to Hawaii, specifically found in the islands' tropical and subtropical regions. It has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Paspalum nesiotes include its ability to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and minor burns, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, Paspalum nesiotes is often used in topical creams and salves to harness its medicinal properties, and it can also be consumed as a tea or infusion to promote overall well-being.
